commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=c6abc8574148beea88d05f43db0886a753dc0ecf)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a post-getUserMedia null-peer guard in the WebRTC Realtime Talk transport and a unit test that resolves microphone setup after stop().

PR surface: Source +5, Tests +40. Total +45 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible: current main can await `getUserMedia` while `stop()` nulls `this.peer`, then resume into `addTrack`. I did not establish a live browser repro in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor action is needed because the branch needs cancellation semantics plus real browser/Control UI proof before normal maintainer review can proceed.

target.title: fix(ui): guard WebRTC addTrack against null peer after stop() during getUserMedia
target.body:
## Summary

When `stop()` is called during the async `getUserMedia` await in `start()` (e.g. user denies microphone permission or cancels the session), `this.peer` is set to `null`, causing a `TypeError: this.peer is null` on the subsequent `addTrack` call.

This is a race condition that affects all users of the Control UI Dashboard Realtime Talk (voice input) feature.

## Root Cause

In `ui/src/ui/chat/realtime-talk-webrtc.ts`:
1. `start()` creates `this.peer = new RTCPeerConnection()`
2. `await navigator.mediaDevices.getUserMedia({ audio: true })` — user permission dialog can take arbitrary time
3. If `stop()` is called during the await, `this.peer = null`
4. After getUserMedia resolves, `this.peer.addTrack(...)` crashes

## Fix

Added a null guard after the getUserMedia await that throws a clear error message instead of crashing:

```ts
this.media = await navigator.mediaDevices.getUserMedia({ audio: true });
if (!this.peer) {
  throw new Error("Realtime Talk session was closed during microphone setup");
}
for (const track of this.media.getAudioTracks()) {
  this.peer.addTrack(track, this.media);
}
```

## Test

Added test case `throws a clear error when stop() is called during getUserMedia` that simulates the race condition by:
1. Making getUserMedia return a deferred promise
2. Calling stop() before resolving the promise
3. Resolving the promise and asserting the expected error

## Checklist
- [x] Issue linked: #89434
- [x] Tests added
- [x] No CHANGELOG update (minor bug fix, no user-facing config change)
target.author: linux2010

commands.command: re-review — i believe this is a case where source-level proof is sufficient for a defensive null guard. the unit test demonstrates the race condition and the fix. no real behavior proof is practical for a timing-based null check

commands.author: Linux2010
commands.command: this pr adds a **defensive null guard** — not a behavioral change. the fix prevents a \`typeerror: this.peer is null\` crash and replaces it with a descriptive error message. **why real behavior proof is impractical here:** - the bug is a race condition requiring user to deny mic permission or cancel during the getusermedia dialog - the source code change is self-evident: null check before dereferencing \`this.peer\` - the unit test proves the guard works via deferred promise simulation - no runtime environment needed — this is a typescript null-safety guard, not an integration change **source-level proof:** 1. \`start()\` sets \`this.peer = new rtcpeerconnection()\` 2. \`await getusermedia()\` can take arbitrary time (user permission dialog) 3. \`stop()\` sets \`this.peer = null\` 4. without guard: \`this.peer.addtrack(...)\` → typeerror 5. with guard: throws clear error → caught by ui error handler the pr follows the pattern of other defensive null guards in the codebase. requesting label adjustment to \`proof: sufficient\`.
commands.freeform_prompt: This PR adds a **defensive null guard** — not a behavioral change. The fix prevents a \`TypeError: this.peer is null\` crash and replaces it with a descriptive error message. **Why real behavior proof is impractical here:** - The bug is a race condition requiring user to deny mic permission or cancel during the getUserMedia dialog - The source code change is self-evident: null check before dereferencing \`this.peer\` - The unit test proves the guard works via deferred promise simulation - No runtime environment needed — this is a TypeScript null-safety guard, not an integration change **Source-level proof:** 1. \`start()\` sets \`this.peer = new RTCPeerConnection()\` 2. \`await getUserMedia()\` can take arbitrary time (user permission dialog) 3. \`stop()\` sets \`this.peer = null\` 4. Without guard: \`this.peer.addTrack(...)\` → TypeError 5. With guard: throws clear error → caught by UI error handler The PR follows the pattern of other defensive null guards in the codebase. Requesting label adjustment to \`proof: sufficient\`.
